payload.summary: Reviewed all three amendments against the actual source code in fs.rs and service.rs. Amendment fr-16-8d96fcd0 identifies a legitimate diagnostic improvement where a contract violation returns a misleading CorruptRecord error. Amendment fr-16-c6b392f9 mischaracterizes a deliberate, well-documented safety deviation as unimplemented — the code and tests explicitly document why legacy None-provenance rows are not backfilled. Amendment fr-16-ec78c103 identifies a real control-flow bug where an early error return at line 3165-3176 prevents fallthrough to the terminal replay matcher when an open runless row has a mismatched started_at.

votes.rationale: The amendment correctly identifies that the contract violation guard at fs.rs:2838-2844 returns None, which causes callers to surface a CorruptRecord error with a misleading message about 'no matching task run' rather than surfacing the actual bug: run_id was supplied without started_at. The current code does prevent the unsafe bypass (good), but the error observed by operators/developers describes data corruption when the real problem is a programming contract violation. Changing the return type to AppResult<Option<usize>> would make this self-describing. This is a genuine robustness improvement — the kind of diagnostic clarity that saves hours during incident debugging. The risk is low (it only changes behavior when a contract is violated, which should be never in correct code) and the benefit is clear.

The amendment claims Issue 3 is unimplemented and tests mask a regression. After reading the code, this is incorrect. The code at fs.rs:2608-2622 contains a detailed comment explicitly explaining the deliberate deviation: legacy entries with snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation == None are ambiguous (could be pre-plan or pre-provenance-tracking) and backfilling them risks silently relabeling with a wrong plan version. The safe_plan_hash_backfill function DOES backfill when provenance is available and the snapshot hasn't evolved (lines 2596-2606), covering the primary use case. The new-entry path at fs.rs:3040-3058 deliberately avoids auto-population due to the dispatch-v1/snapshot-v2 race (also well-documented in comments). The tests at service.rs:4727-4817 correctly assert plan_hash is None at start, filled at completion. The test at service.rs:5274-5285 explicitly documents itself as a 'deliberate deviation from Issue 3 acceptance criteria.' Tests at 5635-5844 cover both the fill and skip paths. These tests accurately assert the actual implemented behavior — they are documenting a considered safety trade-off, not masking a regression.

The amendment identifies a real control-flow gap at fs.rs:3165-3176. When there is exactly one open runless row whose started_at does NOT match the request, the code immediately returns a CorruptRecord error without falling through to the terminal replay matcher (lines 3127-3152). Consider this scenario: bead B starts runless at T1, then a retry starts at T2 (creating a new open entry). The T1 attempt actually completes but crashes before persisting. A replay of the T1 completion with a run_id would find the T2 open runless row (the T1 row is still open or was marked as superseded), see started_at mismatch (T2 != T1), and error out at line 3166. It never checks whether a completed terminal row at T1 exists that could be the correct replay target. The partial-write repair contract at service.rs:527-530 explicitly says replaying terminal completion should finish a partially failed write, but this early return prevents that for the started_at-mismatch case. The fix should allow fallthrough to the terminal replay path (or at minimum check for terminal matches) when the single open runless match fails on started_at.
